prerequisites: A running CockroachDB cluster reachable from the connector, with its host address and SQL port (CockroachDB defaults to port 26257).
The name of the target database on the cluster to read from.
A SQL user (username) on the cluster that has privileges to read the target database and its tables.
For a secure cluster, a password set for that SQL user, since password authentication is only supported on secure clusters.
Network connectivity from the connector to the cluster, and, if SSL is enabled, the CA certificate the client needs to validate the cluster's certificate.

authMethods.name: Username and password authentication
authMethods.summary: Authenticate as a SQL user with a username and password. The descriptor exposes username (config) and password (secret) fields, matching CockroachDB password authentication. Password authentication is supported only on secure clusters; the client must still trust the cluster's CA certificate for the TLS connection.

setupSteps.title: Identify the cluster host and port
setupSteps.body: Set host to the address of the CockroachDB node or load balancer, and port to the cluster's SQL port. CockroachDB uses port 26257 by default; use this value when in doubt.

setupSteps.title: Choose the database and SQL user
setupSteps.body: Set database to the name of the CockroachDB database to read from. Set username to a SQL user that can read that database. On secure clusters, set password to that user's password (the password field is the only secret field for this connector).

setupSteps.title: Configure SSL/TLS
setupSteps.body: Use the ssl option to control connection encryption. CockroachDB SQL connections support sslmode values disable, require, verify-ca, and verify-full; verify-full is recommended for production. Note that even with password authentication the client must trust the cluster's CA certificate.

setupSteps.title: Add optional JDBC parameters
setupSteps.body: Optionally set jdbc_url_params to pass extra JDBC connection options to the driver (for example additional SSL or session parameters) as key-value pairs appended to the connection string.
